sprints.label: Ramp & Discovery
sprints.subtitle: May 18 – May 30 — COMPLETE
sprints.goal: Full context, data foundation, and stack decisions before execution.
sprints.deliverable: RevOps baseline, ICP analysis, ORO × Mailchimp cross-reference, stack confirmed, skills infrastructure live.
tasks: Pre-start SEO audit complete — partsbase.com 42/100 (Grade D, SPA root cause)
Stack decisions confirmed: HubSpot CRM + Marketing Hub, Clay, Vector
ORO × Mailchimp cross-reference complete — suppression list + 58,793 acquisition candidates ready
Mailchimp audit — complaint rate 27–30%, 213k dead contacts, suppression plan ready
RevOps baseline: $14.3M ARR, 61× LTV:CAC, 1.3-month CAC payback
ICP mismatch identified — Airlines, Overhaul Shops, Regional Airlines reclassified T1
Site-architecture audit — 83 pages, 3 orphaned routes, SPA = primary SEO constraint
Skills infrastructure live — partsbase-dan/vetted-skills (177 skills), Mission Control OS operational

sprints.label: Sprint 1
sprints.subtitle: Week 1–2 · June 2–13 — Fix the Foundation
sprints.goal: ICP reclassified. GA4 fires correctly on all routes. Baseline metrics exist.
sprints.deliverable: ICP model updated. GTM History Change trigger deployed. Baseline funnel report.
tasks: Update ICP model: reclassify Airlines, Overhaul Shops, Regional Airlines to T1 in icp-brief.md + memory/icp.md
Update Clay table filter logic to reflect T1 reclassification before any seeding
Get GTM container access from Omar Servin or Sergio Corona — hard deadline June 9
Confirm front-end framework with engineering (React / Next.js / Angular)
Deploy GTM History Change trigger — fires page_view on every SPA route change
Verify page_title and page_location populate correctly per route
Validate UTM parameters persist across SPA route changes
Instrument rfq_submitted (server-side + client-side)
Instrument demo_requested, contact_supplier_clicked, part_search_performed
Instrument search_no_results (feeds supplier acquisition weekly)
Instrument aog_inquiry (triggers immediate sales alert)
Validate all events in GA4 DebugView
Set UTM naming convention: Q[N]-[YEAR]-[CHANNEL]-[TYPE]
Pull Google Search Console data — what is actually indexed now?
Document baseline: traffic, conversion rates, lead volume by source
Digital ad spend audit — channel performance and current cost-per-lead

sprints.label: Sprint 2
sprints.subtitle: Weeks 2–4 · June 9 – July 2 — HubSpot Initiation + Vector + Clay
sprints.goal: HubSpot portal provisioned. Vector pixel live. Clay enrichment running.
sprints.deliverable: HubSpot IT approval + contract signed. Vector identifying visitors. Clay → HubSpot push wired.
tasks: Initiate HubSpot IT approval with Omar Servin — Week 1 action
Initiate HubSpot vendor contract with Sergio Corona — Week 1 action
Get quote for HubSpot Enterprise tier
Audit ORO leads data — field mapping for HubSpot leads sync (ORO stays as leads tracker)
Map ORO leads fields → HubSpot contact properties for leads handoff
Demo HubSpot to one sales rep — adoption target is Day 90, not Day 30
Contract and onboard Vector (Mark Lawry · $2k/mo confirmed)
Deploy Vector pixel via GTM
Configure ICP filter in Vector: aviation industry, 10–200 employees, not current customers
Set up Vector retargeting exports to Google / Meta / LinkedIn ad platforms
Validate de-anonymization rate (~37% of visitors identified)
Set up Clay workspace and connect data sources
Configure Clay enrichment waterfall: company → contact → email verification
Build Clay → HubSpot push workflow (enriched contacts auto-create or update)
Seed Clay table with 100–200 aviation contacts — weight Overhaul Shop + Regional Airline alongside MRO/Distributor

sprints.label: Sprint 3
sprints.subtitle: Weeks 3–5 · June 16 – July 2 — Clay + Claude Outbound POC
sprints.goal: One live outbound workflow targeting T1 segments.
sprints.deliverable: 5-step sequence running to MRO, Distributor, Overhaul Shop, Regional Airline contacts.
tasks: Run Clay LinkedIn enrichment on seeded T1 contacts
Run Clay email finder for work emails
Add Claude API column — aviation segment classifier (T1/T2/T3)
Add Claude API column — personalized opening line on Clay output
Apply T1 filter: MRO, Distributor, Overhaul Shop, Regional Airline · 10–200 employees · not on PartsBase · email found
Export T1 (~40–60 contacts) to HubSpot sequence
Load 5-step sequence: Day 0 AI email · Day 2 LinkedIn · Day 5 follow-up · Day 8 LinkedIn msg · Day 12 final
Set up MeetAlfred for LinkedIn automation
Track: open rate, reply rate, meetings booked
Present results to Rodrigo at Day 30 check-in (July 2)

sprints.label: Sprint 4–5
sprints.subtitle: Month 2 · July–Aug 1 — Digital Funnel + Email Campaigns
sprints.goal: Full funnel live. HubSpot CRM configured. First email campaign sent.
sprints.deliverable: Ad → Web → Vector → Clay → HubSpot end-to-end. Mailchimp decommissioned.
tasks: Configure HubSpot CRM: contact records, company records, deal pipeline
Define lifecycle stages: Subscriber → Lead → MQL → SQL → Opportunity → Customer
Connect all web forms to HubSpot with proper field mapping
Set MQL→SQL handoff SLA: SDR responds within 4 hours, AE books demo within 24 hours
Configure HubSpot → ORO leads sync (HubSpot captures, ORO tracks downstream)
Wire Vector output → Clay enrichment trigger
Wire Clay enrichment → HubSpot (enriched contact with segment tag + lead score inputs)
Validate full loop: ad click → landing → Vector/form → Clay → HubSpot record without manual intervention
Complete sender reputation remediation: list hygiene, SPF/DKIM/DMARC in HubSpot, warm-up schedule
Migrate active Mailchimp lists → HubSpot (mapped, segmented, consent-verified)
Decommission Mailchimp sends — HubSpot becomes sole sender
Design first campaign: T1 supplier acquisition (MRO, Distributor, Overhaul Shop, Regional Airline not on PartsBase)
3-email nurture sequence: problem → solution → proof · personalization via Clay segment tags
Set up Profound account — AEO baseline for aviation parts queries
Build HubSpot attribution report: W-shaped (40% first / 20% mid / 40% last)
Configure lead scoring baseline: rfq_submitted +30, demo_requested +30, pricing_page +15, part_search×3 +10, Vector ICP match +20
Set MQL threshold: score > 75 · wire MQL → sales alert

sprints.label: Sprint 6–9
sprints.subtitle: Month 3 · Aug–Sep 1 — Scale + Optimize
sprints.goal: Outbound at 2+ meetings/week. SEO pipeline live. Lead scoring tuned. Pipeline attributed.
sprints.deliverable: Weekly GTM report automated. AI content pipeline producing indexed pages. Sales rep in HubSpot.
tasks: Scale outbound: run full Clay enrichment on all T1 segments
Review 30 days of HubSpot data with sales team
Calibrate MQL threshold against actual MQL → SQL conversion data
Add aviation-segment scoring dimension (MRO, airline, overhaul shop, regional airline, distributor)
Wire expansion scoring: accounts searching new part categories = upsell signal
Pull Google Search Console data post-SPA-fix — what is actually indexed now?
Identify high-intent long-tail searches: part numbers, ATA codes, manufacturer names
Build AI content pipeline: part category pages, ATA code guides, manufacturer spec pages
Target: pipeline built and first 10 pages indexed — 50 pages is Day 180 target
A/B test outbound subject lines and first-email CTAs
Build second campaign: buyer-side nurture (MRO, airline, overhaul shop segments)
Automate lifecycle nurture: new lead → MQL nurture sequence triggers automatically
Get one sales rep actively logging activity in HubSpot — Day 90 adoption target
Build weekly GTM report: pipeline sourced, pipeline influenced, closed-won (automated Monday delivery)
Present to Rodrigo: full funnel from digital spend → pipeline with channel-level attribution

risks.label: GTM container access blocked — GA4 fix can't deploy
risks.mitigation: Hard deadline June 9 — escalate to Rodrigo as business risk if not resolved by then

risks.label: Mailchimp sender reputation too damaged to recover cleanly
risks.mitigation: 213k suppression pending Rodrigo approval. Do not send to dead list. HubSpot warm-up plan ready.

risks.label: ORO leads data needs audit before HubSpot sync
risks.mitigation: Audit ORO leads fields before syncing to HubSpot; ORO continues as leads-only tracker so full migration not required — scope is leads handoff only

risks.label: ICP sequences built before reclassification — wrong segments
risks.mitigation: ICP update is Sprint 1 Day 1 task. Clay seeding blocked until complete.

risks.label: ORO data too dirty to migrate cleanly to HubSpot
risks.mitigation: Audit ORO before any import; field mapping complete before data moves. Start fresh if quality too low.

risks.label: Sales team doesn't adopt HubSpot by Day 90
risks.mitigation: Demo to one rep in Sprint 2. Build for their workflow, not for marketing. Day 90 adoption target is realistic.

risks.label: Revenue concentration — top 3 segments = ~55% ARR
risks.mitigation: Weight outbound toward Overhaul Shop + Regional Airline to diversify while increasing ARPU mix.

risks.label: GDPR non-compliance for EU contacts
risks.mitigation: CookiePro loads before content. Consent-verify all lists before email sends. Scope hreflang for top 5 EU markets.

risks.label: Vector de-anonymization rate below projected ~37%
risks.mitigation: Contractual rate. ICP filter tightens actionable volume regardless of total ID rate.

risks.label: Attribution model built on bad data
risks.mitigation: Not built until GA4 confirmed clean for 90 days. Gate enforced — target date Dec 1.

stack.note: Fix SPA History Change trigger — target June 9. Blocked on container access. Blocks attribution + lead scoring.
Manages contacts, pipeline, and MAP. ORO transitions to leads-only — HubSpot feeds ORO for downstream system tracking. Confirmed May 22. Loop in Sergio Corona for vendor onboarding.
Replaces Mailchimp. Migrate after sender rep remediation. Mailchimp decommission target: Aug 1.
$2k/mo confirmed (Mark Lawry). ~37% visitor ID rate. ICP filtering + ad retargeting exports. Target live: July 2.
GDPR/CCPA required — 217 countries. Must load before content.
T1 ICP filter updated before seeding (MRO, Distributor, Overhaul Shop, Regional Airline). Target live: July 2.
Segment classifier + personalization layer running on Clay output for outbound sequences.
LinkedIn sequences alongside Clay + Claude email outreach. Phase 2 after Clay POC live.
tryprofound.com — AI search visibility for aviation queries. Early mover advantage. Target: Day 60 (Aug 1).
Needs SPA tracking fix first. AI content pipeline target: 10 pages Day 90, 50 pages Day 180.
A/B testing for campaigns and landing pages. Phase 2.
HubSpot + GA4 data source. Needs 90 days clean data — available Dec 1.

decisions.rationale: Balances discovery (40%) and closing (40%) for B2B marketplace
decisions.impact: Sets Phase 3 measurement framework — not built until Dec 1

decisions.decision: Supplier acquisition as outbound POC target
decisions.rationale: Revenue lever — suppliers pay, buyers search free
decisions.impact: Focuses Clay + Claude POC scope on T1 segments

decisions.rationale: Complaint rate 27–30% vs 4% threshold. 213k dead contacts (52% of list). Immediate remediation required.
decisions.impact: Halts email expansion until list cleaned. Suppression pending Rodrigo approval.

decisions.rationale: Matched ORO portal-account exports against Mailchimp to identify subscribed matches, prospects, cleaned contacts, and protected anti-persona records.
decisions.impact: Suppression list + 58,793 T1 acquisition candidates + 88 anti-persona protects all ready.

decisions.decision: Stack confirmed: HubSpot CRM + Marketing Hub, Clay, Vector
decisions.rationale: Single CRM/MAP platform replacing ORO + Mailchimp. Clay for enrichment. Vector preferred over RB2B for retargeting export capability.
decisions.impact: Tool selection closed. Execution begins June 2.

decisions.rationale: AI-generated answers are a growing buyer discovery channel. Most aviation competitors not invested here yet — early presence compounds.
decisions.impact: Added to Day 60 workstream. Shares signal with SEO content calendar.

decisions.decision: ICP reclassification: Airlines, Regional Airlines, Overhaul Shops → T1
decisions.rationale: RevOps baseline shows these are highest new-cohort ARPU segments ($520–$620/mo, 2.5× T3 average). T3 classification would misdirect outbound from launch.
decisions.impact: Updates icp-brief.md, memory/icp.md, Clay filter, HubSpot sequence targeting. Sprint 1 Day 1 action.

decisions.rationale: May 18–30 used for ramp + discovery. All milestones rebased: Day 30 = July 2, Day 60 = Aug 1, Day 90 = Sep 1, Day 180 = Dec 1.
decisions.impact: Plan v2 and dashboard updated to reflect new timeline.

decisions.decision: HubSpot milestone split: portal by Day 30 · CRM configured by Day 60
decisions.rationale: IT approval + vendor contract + CRM configuration in 30 days from standing start was unrealistic. Portal provisioning is achievable by July 2; full CRM config by Aug 1.
decisions.impact: Day 30 milestone is now 'portal provisioned'. Day 60 milestone adds 'CRM configured'. Sales rep adoption stays at Day 90.

notes.weekNote: Ramp & Discovery complete (May 18–30). Execution Week 1 starts June 2. Clock rebaselined: Day 30 = July 2, Day 60 = Aug 1, Day 90 = Sep 1. Two open blockers entering execution: (1) GTM container access not yet granted — GA4 SPA fix target June 9; (2) Mailchimp suppression pending Rodrigo approval. ICP reclassification (Airlines, Overhaul Shops → T1) must complete before Clay seeding or HubSpot sequences are built.

changelog.message: Dashboard merge complete — Day 4. Mission Control OS + 180-Day Plan unified into CoWork/dashboard.html. Workstreams updated: Tracking → RED (SPA fix pending Day 7), Email Health → RED (Mailchimp sender rep emergency). Week note added.

changelog.author: Codex
changelog.message: Data QA cleanup complete. Overview cards now refresh from Today JSON, Email Health is split from Customer Health Scoring, classification claims use consistent denominators, causality language softened, and attribution timing aligned to the 90-day clean-data dependency.

changelog.author: Claude (scheduled refresh)

Inbox: 0 unread, 3 emails in last 48h — all read. Action items surfaced: (1) Seismic Onboarding Day 3 module overdue since May 20 — complete ASAP; (2) Teams background email from jhernandez — verify name/title on background image. Teams chat pull timed out — mentions field may be stale. Focus blocks updated: morning 8–10am, midday 11am–1pm, afternoon 2:15–5pm. No workstream or risk status changes.

changelog.author: Codex
changelog.message: ORO x Mailchimp findings reconciled. Reframed ORO matches as portal-account matches, corrected source counts (29,997 subscribed ORO matches, 31,443 cleaned, 3,020 unsubscribed, 31,204 not in Mailchimp), repaired t2t3 customer combined CSV to 12,152 rows, and updated dashboard language to avoid active paid customer / consent overclaims.

changelog.author: Dan + Claude
changelog.message: Customer segment intelligence built from ORO exports. Real data: 6,357 active customers, ~$14.3M ARR (revenue period unconfirmed), 490 new customers at $326/mo avg ARPU vs $188/mo legacy base. Tier 1 ICP = Airlines/Overhaul Shops/Regional Airlines (LTV:CAC 186–222×). $10.5M upsell opportunity in existing base (ARPU gap). CEO budget impact brief and segment analysis saved to resources/customerdata/. GTM metrics in Exec View updated with actuals.

changelog.author: Codex
changelog.message: Merged first Exec View scheduled-task output into root dashboard-state.json under exec_view while preserving operational dashboard state. Added toolbox/exec-view-refresh.md to prevent future full overwrites and wrong-path writes.

changelog.author: Claude (exec-view scheduled refresh)
changelog.message: Exec view refreshed — end of Day 5 / Week 1. All source files readable this cycle; source_gaps cleared. Key updates: (1) Stack decisions locked May 22 — HubSpot CRM/MAP, Clay, Vector confirmed. (2) ORO x Mailchimp cross-reference validated with corrected numbers: 29,997 subscribed matches, 31,443 cleaned, 3,020 unsubscribed, 31,204 not in Mailchimp. (3) 88-contact ORO anti-persona protect list confirmed. (4) Two REDs active — Mailchimp suppression blocked on Rodrigo approval; GA4 fix blocked on GTM container access. Decisions_needed updated to reflect stack lock and new ORO account export request.

changelog.author: Dan + Claude
changelog.message: RevOps baseline complete (Day 10). Actuals confirmed from ORO CRM + customer revenue exports: 6,357 active accounts, $14.3M ARR, active ARPU $187.85/mo, new-cohort ARPU $325.77/mo (+73%). LTV:CAC confirmed 61× (corrects earlier 107× estimate). CAC payback 1.3 months. Critical ICP mismatch surfaced: Airlines, Regional Airlines, and Overhaul Shops classified T3 in current ICP model but are the highest new-cohort ARPU segments ($520–$620/mo, 2.5× T3 average). Reclassification required before HubSpot sequence build. Upsell opportunity in existing base: ~$10.5M ARR if legacy accounts reach new-cohort ARPU. Geographic shift: international accounts now 51% of new cohort vs 39% of active base. Site-architecture audit on partsbase.com: 83 pages in sitemap, 3 orphaned routes, SPA routing confirmed as primary SEO constraint. customer_intelligence section added to dashboard state.

exec_view.headline: Week 2: stack confirmed, RevOps baseline set (Amelia verified), ICP mismatch caught. Execution begins June 2. Two items need your decision before we can move.
exec_view.narrative: Discovery is complete. The stack is confirmed (HubSpot, Clay, Vector). Amelia MCP has verified the revenue baseline: $36.8M booked revenue (FY2025, +11% YoY), 6,695 active customers in 217 countries, 2.7% annual churn — producing a confirmed LTV:CAC of 365× at $300 CAC. An ICP mismatch was identified and corrected before it could misdirect the outbound launch. Two items are blocking execution: GTM container access (blocks all tracking and downstream scoring) and Mailchimp suppression authorization (blocks email recovery).

exec_proof_points.item: RevOps baseline confirmed from Amelia MCP live data: $36.8M booked revenue (FY2025), 6,695 active customers, 2.7% churn, 365× LTV:CAC, 1.2-month CAC payback
exec_proof_points.impact: Unit economics are exceptional — 3× stronger than prior estimates based on confirmed 2.7% churn vs 8% assumed. New cohort ARPU $342.63/mo across 395 new customers (L3M). Upsell opportunity quantification pending active base ARPU query.

exec_proof_points.item: ICP mismatch identified and corrected before outbound launch — Airlines and Overhaul Shops reclassified T1
exec_proof_points.impact: Without this fix, HubSpot sequences would have launched targeting the wrong segments. Corrected before any build began.

exec_proof_points.item: Full GTM stack confirmed: HubSpot CRM + Marketing Hub, Clay, Vector — all locked, no further evaluation
exec_proof_points.impact: Tool selection is closed. Execution begins June 2 against a fixed stack.

exec_milestones.milestone: HubSpot IT approval + vendor contract signed → portal provisioned

exec_milestones.milestone: Vector pixel live + Clay enrichment running → first identified visitors flowing into HubSpot

summary: Mailchimp Sender Reputation: RED — Complaint rate 27–30% vs. <4% threshold. 213k dead contacts (52% of list) require suppression. Net list loss: 3,700/mo. Blocked on Rodrigo approval. ORO cross-reference complete: 29,997 subscribed ORO portal-account matches identified, 31,443 cleaned/bounced. Action plan documented at resources/mailchimp-action-plan.md.

GA4 + GTM SPA Fix: RED — page_view never fires on SPA route changes. Blocks HubSpot lead scoring, attribution, and SEO content engine. Day 7 target (May 25) is at risk. Blocked on GTM container access from Engineering/IT.

Stack Decisions Locked (May 22): HubSpot CRM + Marketing Hub confirmed (replaces ORO + Mailchimp). Clay confirmed for data enrichment. Vector confirmed for visitor de-anonymization. These are no longer under evaluation.
HubSpot Setup + Clay POC: AMBER — Planning phase. Onboarding in progress. Day 30 credibility milestone (2+ meetings booked from Clay + Claude outbound) requires HubSpot to be live; clock is running.
MarTech Email Classifier: AMBER — Full spec complete (May 21, §1–§9). Awaiting Dan approval. Block 3 blocked on ORO CRM output JSON schema confirmation from CRM team.

RevOps Baseline (May 27 — Amelia MCP confirmed): $36.8M booked revenue FY2025 (+11% YoY), 6,695 active customers across 217 countries, 2.7% annual churn (was 8% assumed). New cohort ARPU $342.63/mo (395 customers, Feb–May 2026). LTV:CAC 365×, CAC payback 1.2 months. Active base ARPU and gross margin still pending finance confirmation.

ICP Tier Update (Amelia-verified): Contract Manufacturer ($460/mo, n=30), OEM ($435/mo, n=24), and MRO ($416/mo, n=45) are the most reliable Tier 1 targets by sample size and ARPU. Regional Airline has the highest ARPU ($712/mo) but n=4 — directional only. Airline dropped to Tier 3 at $209/mo. All segments still have exceptional LTV:CAC (223×–760×) at $300 CAC — the question is which to weight in paid acquisition and outbound.

risks.title: Mailchimp deliverability in violation — complaint rates 6–7x over threshold
risks.detail: Microsoft O365 at 27.3%, Proofpoint at 30.3%, Cisco Ironport at 32.6%. Threshold is <4%. 213k contacts (52% of 406k list) have not opened in last 50 sends. Net loss 3,700 contacts/month. Zero automations — all sends are manual one-off. Risk: domain blacklisting across B2B inbox providers. 90% of B2B targets are on Microsoft O365.
risks.next_action: Get Rodrigo Garcia approval to suppress 213k dead contacts. Protect 88 ORO anti-persona matches before blanket suppression. Do not send to 3,020 ORO unsubscribed matches.

risks.title: GA4 SPA tracking broken — all downstream MarTech is blind
risks.detail: partsbase.com is a SPA. GA4 page_view never fires on route changes. HubSpot lead scoring, attribution modeling, and SEO content engine are all blocked until this is fixed. Pre-start SEO audit: 42/100. Day 7 target per 180-day plan is May 25 — currently at risk.
risks.next_action: Get GTM container access from Engineering/IT (Omar Servin or Sergio Corona). Confirm front-end framework (React/Next.js/Angular). Deploy History Change trigger. Validate in GA4 DebugView.

risks.title: HubSpot onboarding not yet started — Day 30 POC window closing
risks.detail: HubSpot is confirmed (May 22) but formal IT onboarding has not begun. Clay + Claude outbound POC needs HubSpot live for sequence loading. Day 30 milestone (June 17) requires 2+ meetings booked. Each week of delay compresses the POC window.
risks.next_action: Loop Omar Servin for IT approval. Loop Sergio Corona for vendor contract/onboarding. Seed Clay table in parallel — prompt templates already built.

risks.title: ORO CRM customer data unvalidated — active account status unknown
risks.detail: ORO cross-reference is complete but ORO_Customers.csv confirms portal/login accounts, not active paid customers. 31,443 ORO rows match cleaned/hard-bounced Mailchimp contacts. 31,204 ORO rows are not in Mailchimp at all. Cannot safely activate customer nurture, upsell, or email recovery workflows until subscription/account status is joined.
risks.next_action: Request account/subscription export from Sergio/IT/Sales Ops keyed by Site Id or email with active status, plan tier, ARR, renewal date, and consent fields.

risks.title: ICP model mismatch — highest-value segments targeted as T3
risks.detail: Airlines, Regional Airlines, and Overhaul Shops are classified T3 in the current ICP framework. RevOps analysis (May 27) shows these are the highest new-cohort ARPU segments: Regional Airline $620/mo, Overhaul Shop $581/mo, Airline $520/mo — all 2.5× T3 average and 60–90% above T1 MRO/Distributor segments. Current ICP tier directs outbound effort and sequence content away from the highest-value segments. If HubSpot sequences are built before reclassification, targeting is misconfigured from launch.
risks.next_action: Reclassify Airlines, Regional Airlines, and Overhaul Shops to T1 in ICP framework (resources/icp-brief.md, memory/icp.md). Update Clay table filter logic before first POC sequence run.

risks.title: Revenue concentration risk — top 3 segments are ~55% of ARR
risks.detail: MRO, Distributor, and Airline-type segments collectively represent approximately 55% of total ARR. Concentration in 3 segment types creates exposure if any single segment contracts. Expansion into underrepresented high-ARPU segments (Overhaul Shop, Regional Airline) diversifies the base and increases average ARPU simultaneously.
risks.next_action: Factor concentration into HubSpot sequence prioritization — weight Overhaul Shop and Regional Airline prospecting alongside MRO/Distributor to reduce concentration while improving ARPU mix.

decisions_needed.decision: Authorize Mailchimp mass suppression of 213k dead contacts
decisions_needed.context: Complaint rate is 27–30% against a <4% threshold — domain blacklisting risk is active. Suppression is irreversible and will reduce list size by 52%, but that list is already dead. High performers (Mike - Renewals, PBExpo) are unaffected. 88 anti-persona contacts will be protected before execution.

decisions_needed.decision: Grant GTM container access for GA4 SPA fix
decisions_needed.needed_from: Omar Servin or Sergio Corona (Engineering / IT)
decisions_needed.context: Without GTM container access, GA4 never fires on SPA route changes. This blocks HubSpot lead scoring, attribution, and all SEO measurement. Implementation spec is ready — this is a 1-day deploy once access is granted.

decisions_needed.decision: Approve HubSpot vendor onboarding — IT approval + contract process
decisions_needed.needed_from: Omar Servin (IT approval) + Sergio Corona (vendor contract)
decisions_needed.context: HubSpot manages contacts, pipeline, and Marketing Hub (replaces Mailchimp). ORO transitions to leads-only — HubSpot will feed leads to ORO for downstream tracking. Contract and IT approval required before portal setup. Target: CRM live by July 2.

decisions_needed.decision: Approve S&M budget increase at Day 90 check-in (July → September)
decisions_needed.context: Magic Number is ~29:1 (you generate $29 in annual ARR per $1 of S&M spend). At $17,500/month, the business is massively underinvesting relative to its unit economics (LTV:CAC 365–490×, CAC payback 1.2 months). The Day 90 check-in is the right moment to present 90-day actuals and make the budget case. Build the slide starting Day 60.

priorities_7day.action: Reclassify Airlines, Regional Airlines, and Overhaul Shops from T3 to T1 in ICP model (resources/icp-brief.md, memory/icp.md). Update Clay table filter logic before any HubSpot sequences are built — wrong tier = wrong outbound from launch.
Get Rodrigo Garcia approval for 213k Mailchimp suppression; protect 88 ORO anti-persona matches before executing
Get GTM container access; confirm front-end framework with engineering; deploy and validate GA4 History Change trigger
Initiate HubSpot IT approval with Omar Servin; loop Sergio Corona for vendor contract/onboarding
Request ORO account/subscription export (active status, plan tier, ARR, renewal date, consent fields) from Sergio or Sales Ops — needed to validate $14.3M ARR figure and confirm active vs churned
Seed Clay table with 100–200 aviation parts supplier contacts — weight Overhaul Shop + Regional Airline alongside MRO/Distributor based on ICP reclassification
Review and approve MarTech Email Classifier spec (ai-builds/outputs/spec-martech-email-classifier-2026-05-21.md)

proof_points_recent.item: ARPA gap diagnosed via Amelia SKU-level analysis: mix shift confirmed (not price erosion). BAS-001 User License $73.90 new vs $118.29 renewal. BAS-009 Inventory Mgmt penetration 23% new vs 61% renewal — closes as customers mature.
proof_points_recent.impact: ARPA compression risk is lower than feared — it is a lifecycle/maturity gap, not a pricing problem. HubSpot upgrade trigger (Day 30–60) + guided onboarding for BAS-009 is the right fix. Day 30 deliverable complete.

proof_points_recent.item: SaaS health report run against live Amelia data: active base ARPA $459.99/mo confirmed · MRR $2.37M · Quick Ratio 5.4:1 · Magic Number 29:1 · NDR ~96.5% (WATCH) · LTV:CAC 365–490× · 3 priority issues surfaced
proof_points_recent.impact: Active base ARPA now confirmed (was 'pending'). Magic Number 29:1 quantifies the Day 90 budget increase case. NDR gap ($3.2K expansion vs $9.7K churn MRR) identifies the HubSpot upgrade trigger as the highest-value Day 30-60 build. ARPA gap (new cohort $342 vs base $460) flagged for monitoring to prevent long-term ARPA compression.

proof_points_recent.item: RevOps baseline verified from Amelia MCP live data: $36.8M booked revenue FY2025, 6,695 active customers, 217 countries, 2.7% annual churn, LTV:CAC 365×, CAC payback 1.2 months, new cohort ARPU $342.63/mo (395 customers, L3M)
proof_points_recent.impact: Amelia confirms unit economics are 3× stronger than prior ORO-based estimates. Churn at 2.7% (vs 8% assumed) triples LTV. All downstream GTM files updated to Amelia figures.

proof_points_recent.item: ICP mismatch identified: Airlines, Regional Airlines, Overhaul Shops are highest new-ARPU segments (2.5× T3 average) but currently classified T3 in the outbound model
proof_points_recent.impact: Prevents misconfigured HubSpot sequences from launching against the wrong tier. Correcting before build saves weeks of rework post-launch.

proof_points_recent.item: Site-architecture audit on partsbase.com: 83 pages mapped, 3 orphaned routes identified, SPA routing confirmed as primary SEO constraint
proof_points_recent.impact: Confirms GA4 fix must precede any content investment. Structural baseline ready for post-fix SEO content strategy.

proof_points_recent.item: Core stack locked: HubSpot CRM + Marketing Hub, Clay, and Vector all confirmed — not under evaluation
proof_points_recent.impact: Ends tool-selection overhead. Team can execute against a fixed stack starting Week 2.

proof_points_recent.impact: Suppression list, Tier 1 acquisition candidate list (58,793 contacts), and 88-contact ORO protection list are all ready for activation pending leadership approval.

proof_points_recent.item: Mailchimp full audit completed — complaint rates, dead contact volume, top performers, and segment taxonomy documented
proof_points_recent.impact: 213k suppression target quantified. Remediation plan ready to execute on approval. Mike - Renewals and PBExpo segments confirmed as protected high-value assets.

customer_intelligence.source: Amelia MCP — live Athena CRM/subscription data (pai_analytics_layer)
customer_intelligence.confidence: 🟢 Customer counts, churn, new cohort ARPU, active base ARPU, MRR, Quick Ratio confirmed from Amelia · 🟡 LTV uses confirmed churn (2.7%) + assumed gross margin (72%) · 🟡 NDR/churned MRR estimated (ARPU proxy) · 🔴 Gross margin needs finance confirmation
customer_intelligence.arpu_active_monthly_source: Amelia crm_account.last_renew_price / 12 · 5,149 accounts with price · confirmed 2026-05-27
customer_intelligence.arpu_gap_note: New cohort ARPA ($342.63) is 26% below active base ($459.99) — monitor for ARPA compression as new cohorts renew
customer_intelligence.mrr_note: 1,546 Ordered accounts have no last_renew_price (new, pre-renewal) — MRR floor is $2.37M from renewing base
customer_intelligence.quick_ratio_note: (New MRR $49,152 + Expansion MRR $3,198) / Churned MRR $9,660 = 5.4 — EXCELLENT
customer_intelligence.magic_number_note: Net New ARR (~$512K) / S&M spend ($17,500) = ~29:1 — justifies budget increase pitch at Day 90
customer_intelligence.ndr_note: Estimated: expansion ($3,198/mo) covering only 1/3 of churn ($9,660/mo) — WATCH · target: get to 100%+ via HubSpot upgrade trigger
customer_intelligence.upsell_opportunity_note: 1,546 Ordered accounts at $342.63/mo new cohort ARPA → active base ARPA $459.99 = $117.36/mo × 12 × 1,546 = $2.18M ARR opportunity

geographic_mix.note: International share growing in new cohorts — requires ICP model to explicitly segment US vs international targeting in HubSpot

site_architecture_audit.primary_constraint: SPA routing — GA4 page_view never fires on route change. All content investment is unmeasurable until GA4 History Change trigger is deployed.
site_architecture_audit.secondary_findings: URL structure generally clean. Internal linking gaps in /solutions and /products sections. No breadcrumbs on deep pages. Hub pages (solutions index) have thin content.

customer_intelligence.arpa_gap_note: New cohort ARPA ($342.63) is 26% below active base ($459.99). Diagnosed 2026-05-27: mix shift (not price erosion). Two drivers: (1) BAS-001 User License new=$73.90/mo vs renewal=$118.29/mo — 60% gap, reflects shorter contract tenure; (2) add-on penetration gap — BAS-009 Inventory Mgmt 23% new vs 61% renewal, BAS-003 US Search 10% vs 26%, BAS-005 Batch Search 0.8% vs 9.4%. ARPA gap closes naturally as customers mature. Accelerate via HubSpot upgrade trigger + guided onboarding for BAS-009.
customer_intelligence.arpa_gap_primary_driver: Add-on penetration gap: BAS-009 Inventory Mgmt (23% new vs 61% renewal), BAS-003 US Search (10% vs 26%), BAS-005 Batch Search (0.8% vs 9.4%)
customer_intelligence.arpa_gap_secondary_driver: BAS-001 User License pricing: $73.90/mo new vs $118.29/mo renewal — reflects contract tenure + price escalation, NOT discounting on same tier
customer_intelligence.arpa_expansion_opportunity: If BAS-009 penetration for new cohort rises from 23% to 40%, estimated +$23K/mo MRR from add-on upsell in new accounts
